description: Known cyber-physical attack patterns for SDV threat analysis — aligned with ISO 21434 TARA

attack_patterns.name: CAN Bus Spoofing
attack_patterns.description: Attacker injects forged CAN messages to override brake/steer commands
attack_chain: Attacker gains OBD-II physical access OR infotainment bridge
Injects forged CAN frames with valid IDs (e.g. 0x1A0 BRAKE_CMD)
ECU accepts spoofed message — actuator receives wrong command
Vehicle brakes unexpectedly or fails to brake
attack_patterns.mitigation: CAN message authentication (AUTOSAR SecOC), CAN FD with MAC

attack_patterns.name: Sensor Data Injection
attack_patterns.description: Fake sensor readings injected into perception pipeline to trigger false brake/steer events
attack_chain: Attacker spoofs LIDAR or camera data stream
Perception ECU receives false obstacle data
ADAS_ECU triggers emergency brake for non-existent obstacle
Unexpected vehicle stop causes rear collision
attack_patterns.mitigation: Sensor data validation, redundant sensing, anomaly detection

attack_patterns.name: ECU Timing Attack (Delay Injection)
attack_patterns.description: Attacker deliberately delays ECU processing to cause timing violations in safety-critical loops
attack_chain: Attacker exploits software vulnerability in ECU firmware
Injects high-load processes or sleep calls into ECU task scheduler
Safety-critical task execution delayed beyond ASIL threshold
Timing violation causes functional safety failure
attack_patterns.mitigation: Secure ECU scheduling, RTOS isolation, watchdog timers

attack_patterns.name: OTA Firmware Tampering
attack_patterns.description: Malicious firmware delivered via compromised OTA update server
attack_chain: Attacker compromises OTA update server or man-in-the-middle
Tampered firmware image delivered to vehicle
ECU installs malicious code without signature verification
Attacker has persistent code execution on safety-critical ECU
attack_patterns.mitigation: Code signing, secure boot, HSM-verified firmware, rollback protection

attack_patterns.name: Infotainment to CAN Bridge Attack
attack_patterns.description: Exploit infotainment system as entry point to safety-critical CAN bus
attack_chain: Attacker exploits browser/media vulnerability in infotainment
Pivots from infotainment network to internal CAN gateway
Accesses safety-critical CAN bus (brakes, steering)
Remote control of vehicle dynamics achieved
attack_patterns.mitigation: Network segmentation, CAN firewall, IDS on gateway

attack_patterns.name: Replay Attack on Safety Messages
attack_patterns.description: Attacker records and replays old safety-critical CAN messages at wrong time
attack_chain: Attacker sniffs CAN bus during normal operation
Records valid brake/steer messages
Replays old messages at critical moment (e.g., at high speed)
Vehicle executes outdated command — unsafe state
attack_patterns.mitigation: Message counters, timestamps, freshness tokens in CAN frames

attack_patterns.name: DoS on CAN Bus
attack_patterns.description: CAN bus flooded with error frames or high-priority messages, starving safety functions
attack_chain: Attacker floods CAN bus with high-priority error frames
Legitimate safety messages delayed or dropped
Braking system cannot receive timely commands
Timing violation — vehicle fails to respond
attack_patterns.mitigation: CAN bus load monitoring, error frame rate limiting, redundant Ethernet path

attack_patterns.name: GPS Spoofing for Navigation Hijack
attack_patterns.description: Fake GPS signals mislead navigation and ADAS positioning
attack_chain: Attacker broadcasts stronger GPS signals near vehicle
Vehicle navigation locks to fake position
ADAS map-based features make wrong decisions
Vehicle directed into unsafe route
attack_patterns.mitigation: Multi-constellation GNSS, signal authentication, sensor fusion cross-check
